baselines.note: Without prompt engineering; rises substantially with a single-sentence prefix. Demonstrates probabilistic recall is sensitive to elicitation.
Aggregate across needle depths; recall degrades non-monotonically as depth and context length both grow.
baselines.source: Greg Kamradt, "Pressure Testing GPT-4-128K With Long Context Recall", 2023-11

baselines.note: Headline result; published recall on multi-needle and multi-modal NIAH variants is lower.
baselines.source: Google DeepMind, "Gemini 1.5: Unlocking multimodal understanding across millions of tokens of context", 2024-02

baselines.note: Showed that most open-source models' *effective* context length is far smaller than their advertised length. Top open models (Llama-3.1-70B, Qwen2-72B) drop below 80% accuracy well before nominal context limit.
baselines.source: Hsieh et al., "RULER: What's the Real Context Size of Your Long-Context Language Models?", arXiv:2404.06654, 2024-04

core_guarantee.recall_kind_note: Recall is an exact scan over `cga_inner(query, stored_i)`. There is no approximate index, no embedding compression, no attention bottleneck. The recall pct is 100 by construction at every N; the latency curve (linear) is what's measured.

transformer_baselines.note: Frozen citations of published transformer long-context recall numbers. The baselines here are headline figures reported by the model vendors / published benchmarks; they are NOT re-measured here. They serve as the comparator for ADR-0045: CORE's vault recall is exact-by-construction, the transformer baselines are probabilistic and degrade past each model's effective context. Update only when new published numbers warrant.
